Text Arena (LMArena/Arena.ai) crowd-sourced Elo rating, confirmed directly on the live arena.ai/leaderboard page: 1505 +/- 8, rank #1 overall in the text arena as of the July 10, 2026 snapshot (also #1 in the 'Expert' and 'Math' subcategories). Cross-corroborated by an independent secondary write-up (quasa.io) citing the identical 1505 +/- 8 figure from 7,252 votes. Note for context (not added as a separate figure since it is the same underlying model/leaderboard, just an earlier point in time): Fable 5 briefly peaked near ~1525 Elo immediately after its June 9, 2026 launch before a June 12-13, 2026 US export-control-driven worldwide suspension of non-US access disrupted voting volume; access was restored July 1, 2026, and the July 10 snapshot value used here reflects the stabilized post-restoration rating.

Live Text Arena leaderboard, model row 'claude-opus-4-1-20250805' (non-thinking-tagged variant, matching this corpus record's single isDefaultConfig/isBestConfig entry), Elo 1447 (rank 56/374, 77,265 votes) as of the Jul 13, 2026 snapshot fetched directly from arena.ai. A separate 'claude-opus-4-1-20250805-thinking-16k' row (Elo 1449) also exists but was not used since this corpus record does not track a distinct extended-thinking config. Not previously tracked for this model.

Live LMArena (Chatbot Arena) text Elo, read from the current (mid-2026) leaderboard snapshot: 1396±4, rank ~130 of tracked models, 45,480 votes. Elo is dynamic and decays in relative rank as newer frontier models are added — this is the model's current standing, not a fixed launch-week score. (DeepSeek's own announcement threads separately claimed a #5 rank / #1-open-model position on the arena shortly after the March 2025 launch, and a #4 WebDev Arena ranking, but did not publish a specific Elo number, so those claims aren't independently reproducible as a score here.)

Live LMArena (Chatbot Arena) text Elo, current mid-July 2026 leaderboard snapshot, row explicitly labeled 'deepseek-v3.1' (rank #100 of 374, 1418±6, 14,945 votes) — kept distinct from the separately-listed 'deepseek-v3.1-terminus-thinking' row (rank #99, 1418±10) which is a different reasoning-mode/version entry. LMArena Text leaderboard Elo score for the ERNIE-5.0-Preview-1203 checkpoint (posted by the official @arena/LMArena account), a +23pt update over the earlier -1103 checkpoint; ranked #1 among Chinese models and #2 globally on the Text leaderboard at the time. Corroborated by Baidu's own ERNIE blog post (https://ernie.baidu.com/blog/posts/ernie-5.0-preview-1203-release-on-lmarena/). Not previously tracked -- existing results for this model (gpqa, hle, ifbench, mmmu-pro) are all from artificialanalysis.ai's Intelligence Evaluations breakdown; this is a genuinely new benchmark/slug (LMArena Elo) from a genuinely new, independent source.

gemma-2-9b-it LMSYS Chatbot Arena Elo rating (95% CI +3/-5), from Table 14 of the official Gemma 2 technical report ('Evaluation of Gemma 2 Instruction Tuned models on the Chatbot Arena'). Text confirms: 'Gemma 9B (Elo 1187) similar as GPT-4-0314 (Elo 1186)'. Verbatim-confirmed via ar5iv full-text render of the arxiv mirror (2408.00118, submitted 2024-07-31). Live/current lmarena.ai leaderboard entries were not used since the score there drifts over time and the specific historical launch-week figure is the citable, stable data point.

LMArena (Chatbot Arena) Elo score for Grok 3, per xAI's official launch blog: 'Grok 3 has leading performance across both academic benchmarks and real-world user preferences, achieving an Elo score of 1402 in the Chatbot Arena.' First model ever to break 1400 Elo; ran under early preview codename 'chocolate' before shipping as Grok 3 Beta (confirmed matching score independently reported by Analytics Vidhya and other outlets covering the Feb 2025 LMArena leaderboard).

LMSYS Chatbot Arena Elo, #1 at launch [Corrected during full-corpus audit: xAI's own launch blog states verbatim: 'Grok 3 has leading performance across both academic benchmarks and real-world user preferences, achieving an Elo score of 1402 in the Chatbot Arena.' The existing corpus value of 1400 comes from a Musk quote relayed via rdworldonline.com ('achieve a score of 1,400'), which is a rounded/misquoted version of xAI's own precise, primary-source figure of 1402.]
